perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees that it shall not, and shall cause it Representative not to, remove, overprint or deface any notice of confidentiality, copyright, t rademark, logo, legend or other notices of ownership or confidentiality from any original or copies of the Confidential Information it obtains from Disclosing Party, or authorize any third party to do any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party agrees that it shall not, and shall cause it Representative not to, remove, overprint or deface any notice of confidentiality, copyright, t rademark, logo, legend or other notices of ownership or confidentiality from any original or copies of the Protected Information it obtains from Disclosing Party, or authorize any third party to do any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information'. This creates ambiguity because 'Protected Information' isn't defined elsewhere, leading to uncertainty if the obligations apply only to what's explicitly labeled 'Protected Information' or still to 'Confidential Information' as defined in Section 1. This can create a loophole where obligations regarding 'Confidential Information' are circumvented by arguing it's not 'Protected Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receivi ng Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, immedi ately return (or, at the op tion of Disclosing Party, destroy) all tangible or electronic Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving P arty or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductio ns of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans , enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Part y further agrees to promptly provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or co ntrol of any Confidential Information. Provided, however, that Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information, subject to the restrictions on use and disclosure contained in this Agreement, in accordance
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is consistent with the contradicted text also referring to 'Confidential Information'. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information'. Since 'Protected Information' is not defined, it creates ambiguity as to whether the obligations related to returning or destroying information apply only to what is explicitly labeled 'Protected Information' or also to the 'Confidential Information' mentioned in the contradicted text. This introduces a contradiction because the Receiving Party could argue that the requirement to return 'Confidential Information' does not apply if the information is not also labeled as 'Protected Information', thus creating an in-text contradiction. This is an example of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, as specified in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that any disclosure or misappr opriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cau se Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, a grees that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief in addition to other remedies and relief t hat would be available at law or in equity without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting a bo nd. In the event of any action for breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party or its Representatives, th e prevailing party shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses incurred in such action.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that any disclosure or misappr opriation of any of the Proprietary Data in violation of this Agreement may cau se Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, a grees that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief in addition to other remedies and relief t hat would be available at law or in equity without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting a bo nd. In the event of any action for breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party or its Representatives, th e prevailing party shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses incurred in such action.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data'. While 'Proprietary Data' could be seen as overlapping, it is not clearly defined and not synonymous. Given the other instances of 'Confidential Information', it introduces confusion regarding what the injunctive relief applies to, weakening the enforceability of this clause. The rest of the document uses 'Confidential Information' creating contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All obligations of Receiving Party with respect to any Conf idential Information received from Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement shall s urvive and continue for five (5) years after the date of the last discl osure of Confidential Informati on by Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' introduces a strong contradiction because the contradicted text specifically refers to 'Confidential Information'. Since 'Proprietary Data' is not clearly defined in the original or changed text, and 'Confidential Information' is used elsewhere in the contract (as highlighted in the perturbation explanation), it creates uncertainty about which information the survival clause applies to, thus contradicting the original intent and creating potential unenforceability. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: All obligations of Receiving Party with respect to any Conf idential Information received from Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement shall s urvive and continue for five (5) years after the date of the last discl osure of Confidential Informati on by Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All obligations of Receiving Party with respect to any Private Information received from Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement shall s urvive and continue for five (5) years after the date of the last discl osure of Private Information by Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Information'. Because 'Private Information' is not defined elsewhere in the document, but Confidential Information is, it is ambigious on what is applicable in the text. This can limit the reach of obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receivi ng Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, immedi ately return (or, at the op tion of Disclosing Party, destroy) all tangible or electronic Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving P arty or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductio ns of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans , enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Part y further agrees to promptly provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or co ntrol of any Confidential Information. Provided, however, that Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information, subject to the restrictions on use and disclosure contained in this Agreement, in accordance
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information' creates an in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text refers to 'Confidential Information' regarding the return or destruction of tangible items and the obligation of the Receiving Party to certify the return of 'Confidential Information'. Since 'Private Information' is not defined, the Receiving Party's obligations regarding the handling of 'Confidential Information' become ambiguous. The original text clearly defines these obligations for 'Confidential Information'. The change thus creates a direct conflict, because the Receiving party no longer knows the extent of obligations, especially since 'Private Information' is not defined.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be waived, amended or modified only by an instrum ent in writing signed by the party against which such waiver, amendment or modification is sought to be enforced. The term “person” as used in this Agreement shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any individual, group or entity. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereo f preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereun der. This Agreement is not assignable and is binding on any successors of Receiving Party. If any provis ion of this Agreement is for any reason adjudged to be void, invalid or unenforceable, then the remaind er of this Agreement shall continue and remain in full force and effect. This Agreement may be execute d in counterpart by facsimile, PDF or electronic signature, which shall be deemed to be an original f or evidentiary purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be waived, amended or modified only by an instrum ent in writing signed by the party against which such waiver, amendment or modification is sought to be enforced. The term “individual” as used in this Agreement shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any individual, group or entity. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereo f preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereun der. This Agreement is not assignable and is binding on any successors of Receiving Party. If any provis ion of this Agreement is for any reason adjudged to be void, invalid or unenforceable, then the remaind er of this Agreement shall continue and remain in full force and effect. This Agreement may be execute d in counterpart by facsimile, PDF or electronic signature, which shall be deemed to be an original f or evidentiary purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'person' is changed to 'individual'. This introduces a contradiction since the definition of the term 'person' is still reffered in the agreement, leaving a conflict with the definition in the document. The definition is also now inconsistent, as it states individual should be interprested as individual, group or entity. This change causes in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon receipt of any Confidential Information, Receiving Part y shall: (a) only disclose such Confidential Information to those of its Representatives with a need to have access to and knowledge of the Confidential Inform ation for the limited purpose expressly stated herein and not disclose to any other person ex cept with the prior written approval of Disclosing Party; (b) advise any of its Representatives who receive the Confident ial Information of the existence and terms of this Agreement and of the obligations of confidentiality and non- disclosure herein; cause its Representatives to comply with thi s Agreement as if they were parties hereto; and be responsible for a ny breach of this Agreement by its Representatives; (c) use and require its Representatives to use at least the sam e degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as they use to protect the ir own proprietary information, which degree of care shall not be less than reasonable care; an d
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "person" to include individuals, groups, or entities. The changed text redefines 'person' to 'individual' but keeps the definition to include individual, groups or entities. The 'Contradicted Text' uses the term "person", implying that it would include groups or entities, while now the term "individual" is narrowly defined. The definition of Representatives as a subset of Person now leads to confusion and a potential contraction because the agreement refers to the term "person" elsewhere. It is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, as the use of person and individual interchangeably and redefining it causes in-text contradictions
